data.information:
Livestock sheltering is available for those impacted by the Gold Mountain Fire in Ouray County. If you need shelter for livestock, please call the hotline at 970-626-5484 for registration prior to arrival.

Shelter Location:

Montrose County Fairgrounds
1036 North 7th Montrose, CO

Please note the following before bringing animals to the shelter:

• Water your animals before transport.

• Bring feed if you are able.

• All animals will be inspected by a veterinarian upon arrival.

• Owners are responsible for feeding, watering, exercising, and maintaining their animals while they are sheltered.

Instructions:

  • Enter the Montrose County Fairgrounds via the San Juan entrance north of Main Street.

  • Approach the fenced area where the white rows of permanent horse stalls are located, southwest from the San Juan entrance.

  • Enter the fenced area through the gate and continue through the horse stall area to the last three sets of stalls. Barns D, E and I have been designated for sheltering evacuated animals.

name: Willow Fire
address: ~2 miles NW of the Leadville Fish Hatchery

data.evacuation_notes: Lake County Evacuation & Road Closure Map

data.information:
Pets can be taken directly to the Eagle County Animal Shelter at: 1400 Fairgrounds Road, Eagle, CO 81631, per the Lake County Sheriff's Office.

Please call our on-call officer at 970-328-3647 and press 2 to be connected.

If you are in need of supplies like a crate, bowls, bedding, etc, please call the same number and we can coordinate pick up of those items, per the Eagle County Animal Shelter.

child_geo_events.address: 518 S, San Juan, Buena Vista, CO
child_geo_events.name: Darren Patterson Christian Academy

data.information: This shelter will be in standby status at 7pm on 07/09/2026. If you are in need of shelter as a result of the Willow Fire, please report to the location and follow the instructions on site, per the Lake County Office of Emergency Management.

All large animals are safely returning home today (07/12/2026), and the shelter is stepping back to on-call status. If that changes for you, you can call anytime and reach Diana at 719-839-1933 if you have any sheltering needs or questions, per the Lake County Office of Emergency Management.

If you are still within a mandatory evacuation zone, please stop by the Lake County Public Library to pick up your Salamander Card. Please bring a photo ID and proof of residence. The library will be open: Monday - 10:00 A.M. to 6:00 P.M. Tuesday - 10:00 A.M. to 5:00 P.M. Wednesday - 10 A.M. to 6:00 P.M. Thursday/Friday - 10 A.M. to 5:00 P.M. Saturday - 12:00 P.M. to 4:00 P.M. Sunday - CLOSED If you are unable to get to the library due to a significant barrier, please call 719-427-0252, per the Lake County Sheriff's Office

data.information:
As of 11:20 a.m. The large animal/livestock evacuation center is being re-located to the Colorado State Fairgrounds, enter at Gate 7 ONLY off Small Ave.

The site at 4 Bar S will be closing.

-Per Pueblo County Sheriff's Office on Facebook.
An Evacuation Center is open to people and animals in Fremont County at Pathfinder Park (6655 CO-115, Florence, CO 81226), per Custer County Sheriff's Office on Facebook.

An evacuation center is open in Custer County at Lange Hall, 312 S. 3rd Street, Westcliffe, per the Pueblo County Emergency Status Board.

Small animals accepted, per the Custer County Sheriff's Office.
An evacuation center has been set up and is open in Pueblo County at 1650 Cooper Place. Household pets are being accepted. Via Pueblo County Sheriff's Office.

data.information: Beginning Monday, July 20th, a Recovery Assistance Center, (RAC) will be set up at Beulah Heights Elementary, 2670 Delphinium St. The RAC will provide resources and services to those affected by the fire, per the Pueblo County Sheriff's Office.

data.information:
ASHLAND: Due to heavy smoke in the Ashland area, a Cleaner-Air Center will be open in Ashland from 9am-7pm today June 17, 2026

This Cleaner-Air Center is managed by OHRA, Opportunities for Housing Resources and Assistance. Phone#: 541-631-2235

Doors open at 9AM, doors close at 7PM. There is limited space available.

Center is located at 2350 Ashland St, Ashland, OR 97520.

This is a low-barrier, no frills, daytime cleaner-air center for anyone needing to get out of the smoke. Center is coordinated and staffed by OHRA employees and willing volunteers at all times.

Want to receive notifications for shelter and center activations in Ashland?

Text 97520SHELTER to 888777.

Per Jackson Alerts on Everbridge.

links.label: Opportunities for Housing Resources and Assistance

child_geo_events.name: Eagle Point Middle School

data.information:
As evacuation levels continue to decrease, the American Red Cross shelter at Eagle Point Middle School is no longer accepting new evacuees. The Red Cross will continue working with current shelter residents to support their transition. Residents needing sheltering assistance should contact Non-Emergency Dispatch at 541-776-7206. Via ODF Southwest Oregon District Facebook

Pets are welcome at Eagle Point Middle School shelter. They will be sheltered in a separate room within the building.

child_geo_events.address: 601 Billy Ives Track Rd, Omak, WA
child_geo_events.name: Omak Community Center

data.information: Evacuees from the Kaiser Canyon Fire can now take shelter at the Omak Community Center (601 Billy Ives Track Rd, Omak, WA), which is open and ready to receive residents, per the Colville Tribal broadcast system on Facebook.

The Omak Longhouse (25 Mission Rd, Omak, WA 98841) is open for evacuees of the Kaiser Canyon Fire, per Colville Tribes Emergency Management on Facebook.

This shelter accepts pets: Off-site ~Per the Red Cross.

An additional shelter has opened at the Lake Roosevelt High School Gym located at 503 Crest Dr #505, Coulee Dam, WA 99116, per the the Colville Tribal broadcast system on Facebook.

This shelter accepts pets: Cohabited ~Per the Red Cross.
